title: Go 1.26 and AWS SDK for Go v2 Client Contract · Execution Trace: Config Client Lifecycle
summary: A service copies an SDK v1 tutorial, floats module versions, shares request options incorrectly, and treats generated Go types as runtime validation. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the config client lifecycle increment in pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Load immutable shared configuration once, construct scoped clients, pass context, and assign transport and credential lifetimes.
Pin Go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, S3 1.105.0, CloudFront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Config Client Lifecycle · Theory Config Client Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7110 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Config Client Lifecycle contract for the supplied data.
